rationale: The citation is used for more than background: the authors explicitly reanalyze the Yang et al. (2015) eye-tracking conjoint dataset in Study 3. They also rely on Yang et al. as prior evidence that information processing changes across repeated elicitation questions. This gives the cited paper a clear empirical and methodological role in the citing paper's design and argument about adaptation and external validity.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Yang et al. (2015) as a key empirical foundation, especially by reusing its conjoint eye-tracking data and fixation processing setup for new analyses of adaptation and external-validity peaks. It builds its Study 3 tests on that prior study's measurement context rather than merely listing it as related work.
